academic_requirements.summary: Limited-admission bachelor's degree taught in English. Applicants need a secondary school qualification valid for university admission; admission is based on an entry test and, for international applicants, preliminary qualification evaluation through apply.unive.it.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Secondary school leaving certificate eligible for university admission, or a European Baccalaureate/European Secondary School Leaving Certificate obtained from European Schools in Italy. International applicants must normally have completed at least 12 years of schooling.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: No programme-specific subject prerequisites are published, but applicants must sit the programme's entry test and rank successfully for admission.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: The qualification must normally be valid for access to higher education in the country where it was awarded. Applicants with only 10-11 years of schooling must provide additional qualifications covering the 1-2 year gap.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: International applicants first undergo preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it. Applicants with international qualifications can accept a place only if they registered on apply.unive.it by the relevant deadlines and their qualification was deemed valid for admission. The programme then applies ranking-list admission through its entry test.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certificate on Ca' Foscari's AICLU-based list, such as Cambridge FCE, Trinity ISE II, IELTS 5.5-6.5, TOEFL iBT 72-94, or PTE Academic 59-75
A CLA Ca' Foscari Level B2.2 linguistic certificate or certificate of attendance and advancement
A certificate from a university language school included in the recognised-certifications list
An approved exemption-category self-certification supported by the required evidence
exemption_rules: Completed higher education studies, or at least one year of college, in a country where English is the medium of instruction
Obtained a high-school diploma or university qualification from a study path taught entirely in English
Completed at least one full high-school exchange year at an institution where English was the language of instruction
Hold one of the listed Italian university language degrees with a three-year English language course
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Candidates can upload the English B2 certificate or exemption self-certification during selection registration. For enrolment, the relevant documentation must also be present in the enrolment application.
language_requirements.deadline: By the enrolment deadline. In the summer 2026/2027 call, conditional enrolment may be granted if the deadline does not allow recovery of the missing language requirement, but the requirement must then be fulfilled by 25 September 2026.

rounds.repechage_notes: Repechage application: 8-9 September 2026; repechage ranking: 15 September 2026; repechage acceptance deadline: 17 September 2026; further notice on remaining places: 24 September 2026.

application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: 31 July 2026 for spring winners; 3 September 2026 for summer winners, with repechage follow-up deadlines through 24 September 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: These dates apply to applicants not requiring a visa, including EU/Schengen citizens and non-EU citizens already resident in Italy with a valid permit for university enrolment.

rounds.round_name: Admission rounds via apply.unive.it and dedicated visa-applicant test [uncertain]
rounds.notes: [uncertain] The programme's apply.unive.it profile confirms a dedicated online entry test for non-EU visa applicants with one test date per admission round, but the static official pages cited here do not expose the exact 2026/2027 round dates.

application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After admission, begin Universitaly pre-enrolment and the visa process as soon as possible.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa for the 2026/2027 academic year.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: International applicants requiring a visa had to apply through apply.unive.it and then take the dedicated online entry test assigned to their admission round.

required_documents.notes: For selection registration, applicants must enter the details of a valid identification document and upload a copy; accepted examples in the call are identity card, passport, or driving licence.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or, for European applicants, ID card
required_documents.notes: A renewal or first-request receipt is also accepted where stated.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or the Schengen Area; a copy of the residence permit is also uploaded at enrolment for non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Mark sheet or transcript of at least the last year of secondary studies
required_documents.notes: If still in the last year of school, a provisional mark sheet/transcript is still mandatory.

required_documents.document_name: Official English or Italian translations of entry documents
required_documents.notes: Required whenever the original documents are not in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: English B2 certificate or exemption self-certification
required_documents.notes: Uploaded during selection registration; the ranking gives evidence of the evaluation of the submitted language documentation.
This may be provisional at application stage if the final document is not yet available, but the final version must be provided before the end of the enrolment period.

required_documents.document_name: Qualification-recognition documentation not previously produced on apply.unive.it
required_documents.notes: The call requires students with qualifications achieved abroad to upload any documentation they could not produce during the apply.unive.it assessment of admission requirements.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability from CIMEA, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification
required_documents.notes: The general international-entry page explains that one of these routes is needed later to complete enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Online written admission test in English
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants. Applicants not requiring a visa use the spring or summer selection calls; non-EU visa applicants take a dedicated online entry test assigned to their apply.unive.it admission round [uncertain exact 2026/2027 dates on static source].
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Spring selection test: 6 May 2026. Summer selection test: 26 August 2026. Non-EU visa-applicant test dates are round-based and emailed individually [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The test lasts 80 minutes and contains 80 questions: 50 on topical-text understanding and 30 on general knowledge. Scoring is 1 point for each correct answer, 0 for each blank answer, and -0.33 for each incorrect answer.

source_quotes.quote: Bachelor's degree programmes - a.y. 2026/2027 ... Philosophy, International and Economic Studies
This is a limited-admission programme taught in English that requires candidates to take an entry test.
The admission procedure for the 2026/2027 academic year includes two selection rounds: spring and summer.
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory for enrolment.

uncertainty_notes: The official programme home and presentation pages still show 'Enrolment from 2025/2026', but the 2026/2027 central international-programmes list, the 2026/2027 admission page, the 2026/2027 call PDFs, and the 2026/2027 study-plan reference confirm current admission for 2026/2027.

The static official pages do not expose the exact 2026/2027 non-EU visa round dates for this programme; they only confirm that visa applicants use apply.unive.it and a dedicated online entry test, while the generic non-EU visa article currently says applications are closed.
apply.unive.it marketing pages show programme-specific tuition figures that differ from the central 2026/2027 fees page, so only the official central fees link is retained.
